title: Living in Boulder vlog 1 -  25 - 2017: Bi-weekly history test page & Colosseum

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from his Boulder apartment on January 25, 2017, discussing a potential skin cancer spot he plans to have checked, shoulder pain he's experiencing from inactivity, and his excitement about creating a bi-weekly history test page. He also mentions buying MSM and fish oil supplements, trouble with image rotation software while working on a Colosseum tracing project, and considers starting swimming to address his shoulder pain.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Has been avoiding it but recognizes he needs to start, especially swimming, to address worsening shoulder and back pain
Very excited about the project and wants to start it soon
Spent $60 on supplements and is now out of money, feels he needs to get on Patreon
Acknowledges he's taken on too many projects but feels he's made enough progress that even slowing down will eventually get him to his goals
Frustrated that the text-to-speech feature doesn't work on decade pages unlike other Wikipedia articles

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He definitely has skin cancer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has a skin cancer spot right on top of his scalp.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is going to see his doctor on Monday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His Medicaid turned on on the 11th, so luckily it happened at the right time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The skin cancer spot just popped up out of nowhere.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He squeezed the spot and it turned into a big thing with pustules.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wrote a page about the bi-weekly history test.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He will put the link to the page in the comment section for people to read.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He traced a few stairs but couldn't rotate the image he was working with.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When he can't rotate an image, he just turns off his computer and turns it back on.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His shoulders started hurting really bad, possibly due to a latent injury.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He thinks he might have twisted his shoulder kayaking a long time ago.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He tried to bench press 135 pounds without training and it caused pain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His shoulder pain is as painful as any burn when exercising.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has been feeling nerve damage in his lower and mid back.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: His couch has started to sink down, and he has to throw more pillows on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He went to the vitamin cottage store to buy MSM and fish oil for his joints.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He spent $60 at the store and is now out of money.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He needs to get on Patreon to start making money.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to start exercising to avoid feeling pain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is excited about listening to the decade pages on Wikipedia.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The decade pages on Wikipedia don't have a play feature to read the text.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He might convert the decade pages into MP3 files to listen to them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He wants to start swimming because of his shoulder pain.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He has a heated pool at his apartment.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He is not tired at all and wasn't yelling before starting the video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He might start exercising and put his work on the back burner.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He believes he has come far enough that even if he cuts his work down, he will still reach his destination.
